result:
Storage model — one row per card vs ledger of issue/redeem/refund events. Ledger wins if you ever need partial refunds, expiry, or audit.

Balance source of truth — store remaining_balance column, or always recompute from ledger. Recompute is safer, column is faster; most teams cache + reconcile.

Code format + entropy — how long, charset, check digit? Needs enough entropy that codes can't be guessed. Decide if code IS the secret or if there's a separate PIN.

At-rest secrecy — hash codes in DB (like passwords) so a DB leak doesn't hand over live balances. Means lookup by hash, not by code.

Concurrency — two tabs redeeming same card at same time. Row lock, optimistic version, or idempotency key on redeem. Pick one and commit.

Partial redemption semantics — card covers $30 of $50 order. Does card charge before tax, after tax, before shipping? Affects accounting and refund math.

Refund path — customer returns item paid 60% card / 40% credit card. Refund proportionally, card-first, or card-last? Pick rule, document it, because finance will ask.

Multi-card stacking — can user apply 2+ codes per order? If yes, define order of application and UI for it.

Currency + FX — card issued in USD, order in EUR. Freeze FX at redemption time or at issuance? Or restrict card to single currency.

Expiry + dormancy — do cards expire? Many jurisdictions (US states, EU) ban or restrict expiry and dormancy fees. Legal, not just product.

Fraud — velocity limits on redemption attempts, lockout after N bad codes, alerting on bulk redemption from one IP/account. Gift cards are a classic money-laundering + carding target.

Issuance channel — purchased (real money in), promo (marketing grant), refund-as-credit. Each has different tax and revenue-recognition treatment.

Taxability — in most places gift card purchase is not taxed, redemption is. Your tax engine must treat card tender differently from cash tender.

Revenue recognition — liability on balance sheet until redeemed, then revenue. Accounting team needs a report; build it from day one or regret it.

PCI + checkout flow — where in checkout does code entry happen? Before payment method selection so remaining total is known before card auth. Pre-auth for full amount then void is worse.

Idempotency — redeem endpoint must be idempotent on client retry, or a flaky network double-spends the card.

Transferability — is card bound to an account or bearer-instrument? Bearer means whoever has code spends it; account-bound means you need login. Big UX + fraud tradeoff.

First decisions I'd lock before coding: ledger-vs-column, hash-at-rest yes/no, refund rule, expiry policy, single-currency vs multi. Rest can follow.
